feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: 'Europe should evolve its carbon market, not dilute it,' investors say
entry.content: A group of investors managing €11.4 trillion in funds are advocating for complementary industrial policies such as grids, electrification and clean tech financing, reflecting a broader shift in European climate policy from setting targets toward managing industrial transformation.

entry.title: A turning point: Why Germany’s military capabilities are underestimated
entry.content: Many people believe that Germans are not prepared for defence. In a guest article for Euronews, military sociologist Dr Timo Graf uses recent survey data to show why this view underestimates German society’s capacity for defence.

entry.title: Subway station in Mexico City goes viral over chandeliers
entry.content: Decorative chandeliers installed at a busy Mexico City subway station ahead of the 2026 World Cup have sparked a wave of memes and mixed reactions from commuters.

entry.title: These are all the carriers banned from flying in Europe after EU Air Safety List update
entry.content: With the EU’s latest Air Safety List including Air Express Algeria among carriers banned from operating within European airspace, here’s what travellers need to know about how airlines end up on it.

entry.title: Thai woman in custody after US diplomat found dead in Myanmar, State Department says
entry.content: The Thai Embassy in Yangon and Thailand's Foreign Ministry refused to say whether they had provided consular support to the suspect or give any other information.

entry.title: One dead and two missing in missile strike on tanker near Oman
entry.content: The Palau-flagged Settebello transmitted a distress call on Wednesday reporting a missile strike on its engine room off Sohar in the Gulf of Oman, where local authorities are assisting with crew evacuation, the UK maritime agency reported.

entry.title: Philippines continues rescue effort after powerful Mindanao quake
entry.content: The Philippines continued rescue operations on 10 June after a magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck Mindanao, killing at least 37 people, injuring nearly 500 and forcing more than 32,000 residents from their homes.

entry.title: Germany's most expensive hotels: luxury stays up to €53,000 a night
entry.content: In Germany's most exclusive hotels, a night costs up to 53,000 euros. These seven suites are setting new standards in comfort, service and prestige.

entry.title: ‘Europe cannot afford bureaucratic paralysis’: EU strikes defence simplification deal
entry.content: Facing Russian aggression and US retreat, the EU is planning to streamline permits, cut bureaucracy and make defence procurement and transfers across member states easier.

entry.title: Madrid’s Nobu Hotel to open in September with three-storey restaurant from famed Japanese chef
entry.content: Spain’s luxury hotel scene is set for a major addition this September with the grand opening of Nobu Hotel Madrid. Opening ahead of the Formula 1 weekend, the design-led stay promises skyline views, signature Chef Nobu Matsuhisa dishes and a prime location in the capital.

entry.title: Netherlands: Marktweg in The Hague becomes iconic orange street for World Cup fever
entry.content: Residents of Marktweg in The Hague have once again transformed their street into a sea of orange ahead of the FIFA World Cup, continuing a popular local tradition that has become a symbol of community spirit and support for the national team.

entry.title: Defying Russia: Poland and Germany plan massive offshore wind farms in the Baltic Sea
entry.content: Despite hybrid attacks from Russia, Poland is betting on offshore wind power from the Baltic Sea. At a forum in Berlin, one message was clear: the region could become Europe’s next major energy hub. Will Europe seize this opportunity?

entry.title: The world’s best hotel bars have just been named by Forbes – and 13 are in Europe
entry.content: From a bar in a former bank vault to a spot serving up caviar chicken nuggets, these hotel bars are a cut above the rest.

entry.title: Europe’s chaotic Entry/Exit System could take up to two years to stabilise, EU official warns
entry.content: Two months after Europe’s new biometric border checks launched, long queues and inconsistent procedures continue to frustrate travellers. Now, a Frontex official has warned that the troubled Entry/Exit System could take up to two years to fully stabilise.

entry.title: Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan receives warm welcome home after being denied entry to US
entry.content: Andrew Giuliani, who is leading the White House task force for the FIFA World Cup, has said Artan was denied entry to the US for a "very good reason," without offering further details.

entry.title: How AI and satellites can help protect our oceans | Euronews Tech Talks
entry.content: The European Digital Twin Ocean builds on artificial intelligence and satellite technology to create a virtual replica of the ocean for scientists and policymakers. How does it work?

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 10th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 10th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Spain: Pope Leo XIV surprises passengers with cockpit visit on Madrid-Barcelona flight
entry.content: Pope Leo XIV made a rare visit to the cockpit mid-flight before greeting passengers and crew during his flight from Madrid to Barcelona on 9 June, marking the second stage of his week-long visit to Spain.

entry.title: Fake article claims Mbappé made sexual harassment allegations against Macron
entry.content: A report attempting to mimic a legitimate Eurosport article claims that French football sensation Kylian Mbappé has made a series of revelations about inappropriate behaviour by Preisdent Emmanuel Macron.

entry.title: Hungary submits revised EU recovery plan as MEPs demand transparency over €16.4bn in frozen funds
entry.content: Hungary has submitted its revised Recovery and Resilience Plan to unlock billions of frozen EU funds, but MEPs are keen to make sure the post-Orbán disbursement can be properly scrutinised.

entry.title: This new private island retreat in France is taking wellness to the next level
entry.content: This sea-facing escape harmonises holistic wellbeing with Mediterranean dining, which includes personalised consultations, tailored treatments across five wellness programmes and three choices of residence – all in one retreat.

entry.title: Amid outcry against France's courts, who has the most and fewest judges in the EU?
entry.content: Mounting criticism of France's justice system following the murder of a schoolgirl has revived debate over judicial resources. With judges in short supply, how does Europe compare?

entry.title: Lombardy's 'Leonardo ferry' has carried commuters for 500 years
entry.content: The boat designed by Leonardo da Vinci has come back into use after a bridge was closed and traffic increased. The ferry is a model of sustainable transport, powered solely by the river’s current.

entry.title: EU migration rules kick in, but enforcement is already in doubt
entry.content: Two years after it was signed, the EU's Pact on Migration and Asylum takes effect this Friday, and the gap between promise and delivery is already wide. Will it hold? Watch the video.

entry.title: USA: French Air Force display marks 250 years of American independence
entry.content: France's renowned Patrouille de France performed a symbolic flyover above New York on 9 June. The display launched a series of events marking the upcoming 250th anniversary of the United States Declaration of Independence.

entry.title: Former Air Canada captain accused of flying for years without the correct license
entry.content: "Safety was not compromised by this incident because all pilots at Air Canada undergo mandatory recurrent training every six months to validate their flying competency," Air Canada said.

entry.title: Spain: Human castle welcomes Pope Leo XIV to youth vigil in Barcelona
entry.content: Welcomed by a towering Catalan human castle, Pope Leo XIV joined 40,000 young people in Barcelona on 9 June, urging greater support for those facing anxiety, loneliness and violence ahead of the blessing of the Sagrada Família's Tower of Jesus Christ.

entry.title: Newsletter: From K-pop to K2 tanks, South Korea flexes in Europe
entry.content: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung visits the Belgian capital on Wednesday for a highly anticipated EU-South Korea Summit. Onlookers expect a buffet of East Asian strength on display, ranging from K-pop toppers to military might, and closer Brussels-Seoul relations.

entry.title: 'No Lisbon without saints': Popular Saints festivals in the capital
entry.content: The Popular Saints festivities are back in Lisbon, with Saint Anthony celebrated this Saturday as the city turns into a huge street party with music everywhere.

entry.title: Europe Today: US and Iran exchange fresh attacks as EU unveils new Russia sanctions
entry.content: Iran has launched a missile attack on a US air base in Jordan after President Donald Trump blamed Tehran for the downing of an American Apache helicopter. In Europe, Jorge Liboreiro reports on the EU's new package of sanctions against Russia targeting oil, the Russian "shadow fleet", and banks.

entry.title: Two officers injured as anti-immigration protests break out in Belfast following stabbing
entry.content: Police charged a 30-year-old Sudanese man with attempted murder in relation to the knife attack.

entry.title: 'Disclosure Day': Will Steven Spielberg deliver an audacious on-screen alien?
entry.content: Filmmakers have shaped our perception of the unknown, and Steven Spielberg has had aliens on the mind for a while now. He even believes they have been to Earth and may still be amongst us. Will his depiction of alien lifeforms in his anticipated new film 'Disclosure Day' challenge audiences?

entry.title: Exclusive: ‘If China attacks Taiwan, you will be affected too,’ Taiwan’s deputy FM warns Europe
entry.content: Euronews Next sat down with Taiwan’s deputy foreign minister Wu Chih-chung in an exclusive interview to talk about the silicon shield and why the world cannot afford to ignore the political tensions.

entry.title: Mexico City counts down to 2026 World Cup opener at historic Azteca Stadium
entry.content: Mexico City is preparing to host the opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Thursday when the hosts take on South Africa at the iconic Estadio Azteca. The venue will become the first stadium in history to host three World Cup opening matches.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 10th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 10th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: From cranes and castles to boats and igloos, these are the best one-of-a-kind hotels in Europe
entry.content: Tripadvisor has just unveiled its Best of the Best Hotels for 2026, and plenty of the show-stopping options can be found on the continent.

entry.title: World Cup 2026: Market analysts who called it twice have picked a new winner
entry.content: Canadian investment research firm BCA Research has applied the same statistical rigour it uses to analyse global markets to predict the outcome of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which kicks off on Thursday in Mexico City.

entry.title: Cars and homes burn as unrest erupts in Belfast after knife attack
entry.content: Multiple fires burned across parts of Belfast as anti-immigration protests erupted following a stabbing attack. Footage showed burning vehicles, damaged homes and emergency crews responding overnight.

entry.title: Tehran fires missiles at Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain after renewed US strikes on Iran
entry.content: Jordan intercepted five missiles aimed at an area hosting a major airbase near Azraq while Kuwait and Bahrain said their air defences were engaging incoming fire. The attacks followed US strikes on Iranian air defence and radar sites near the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Artemis III: Luca Parmitano selected for next stage of NASA's lunar landing mission
entry.content: NASA has unveiled the Artemis III crew for a proposed 2027 mission to test key Earth-orbit technologies ahead of sending a human crew to the Moon.

entry.title: Murder of Lyhanna: Anger grows as thousands take to the streets in Paris
entry.content: Thousands of people gathered in Paris to demand accountability and reforms after the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, criticising the handling of the case by French authorities and the justice system.

entry.title: US strikes Iran in 'proportional response' to US Apache helicopter crash
entry.content: A military response from the US could threaten the ceasefire that has been in place since 8 April, as Tehran warned "foreign forces" to leave the Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Archaeological sensation: Iron Age Celtic grave discovered in Hesse
entry.content: During surveys for a solar park in Hesse, archaeologists uncovered a Celtic princely tomb with exceptional grave goods near Bad Camberg, a find of European significance, according to Hesse’s state archaeologist Udo Recker.

entry.title: Israeli warnings trigger mass exodus from Lebanon’s historic Tyre
entry.content: Thousands of residents fled Lebanon's southern city of Tyre on 9 June, with long queues of vehicles heading north after Israeli military warnings heightened fears of further air strikes and a wider regional escalation.

entry.title: Leo XIV and Bad Bunny meet at Bernabéu: faith and reggaeton cross paths in Madrid
entry.content: After days of speculation that the Pontiff and the singer might cross paths while both were in Madrid, it has been confirmed that Leon XIV and Bad Bunny met on Monday after the “festival of faith” at the Bernabéu stadium.

entry.title: El Niño is set to bring extreme weather this summer. Here’s what it means for travellers
entry.content: The UN’s World Meteorological Organisation now warns there is an 80% chance El Niño develops this summer.

entry.title: EU targets Russia's Patriarch Kirill in new sanctions proposal
entry.content: Patriarch Kirill, the head of Russia's Orthodox Church, is among the many names included in the latest proposal of EU sanctions against Russia.

entry.title: Spain: Thousands gather as Pope Leo XIV reaches Barcelona
entry.content: Spain's Barcelona welcomed Pope Leo XIV on 9 June as thousands gathered for a prayer service at the city's cathedral of the Holy Cross and Saint Eulalia. The pontiff's visit includes a landmark ceremony at the Sagrada Familia Basilica.

entry.title: These are the best and worst airports in Europe, according to a new analysis
entry.content: The new ranking from AirHelp looks at on-time performance, facilities and overall airport experience.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 9th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 9th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: 'EU's environmental policy must be part of defence strategy,' Commissioner Roswall says
entry.content: The European Environment Commissioner told Euronews that Brussels must "connect the dots" and understand that environmental, economic and security interests go hand-in-hand.

entry.title: Kaja Kallas urges Ireland to clarify alumina sales to Russia
entry.content: Ireland has launched an investigation into allegations that exports from Aughinish Alumina have contributed to Russia's military complex.

entry.title: France bars Israel's Smotrich as others push back against Israeli settlement expansion
entry.content: The move by Paris comes as the UK, Canada and Norway intensify sanctions on settlement-linked organisations and financial networks accused of supporting violence in the occupied West Bank.

entry.title: 'Operation Pushkin': six Georgians on trial in France over theft of rare Russian books
entry.content: The trial is the latest case aimed at delivering justice after a series of similar thefts in libraries across Europe, allegedly by an organised network.

entry.title: Spain’s Sánchez speaks out against EU's deregulation crusade
entry.content: As socialists discuss how to regain ground across Europe, the Spanish prime minister has rejected the idea that fewer laws are needed at the European level, putting him at odds with the simplification push led by Germany and Italy.

entry.title: EU auditors flag weak oversight of €527 million Balkans transport fund
entry.content: The European Commission lacked effective tools to monitor progress and relied on international lenders to oversee progress made in the transport sector in the Balkans, leading to the release of funds beyond the level of work completed, according to EU auditors.

entry.title: How the ICD protecting Christian Eriksen's heart on the football pitch actually works
entry.content: For the second time in his career, Danish footballer Christian Eriksen collapsed on the pitch. Here is how his implantable cardioverter defibrillator works.

entry.title: Replacing US military assets will cost Europeans €500bn, EU defence chief says
entry.content: As the Trump administration continues pulling back from Europe, Andrius Kubilius warned on Tuesday that Europe can only fill the resulting gap with cooperation and a "big spend".

entry.title: Dua Lipa and Charli XCX’s Sicily weddings see searches for the Italian island soar
entry.content: In Italy, Google searches for the singer's wedding location have increased by 94% in the last week.

entry.title: No Glastonbury, no problem: Here are eight European music festivals you can still book this summer
entry.content: Most of Europe's music festivals have sold out, but there are plenty you can still get tickets for across the continent.

entry.title: ‘Act without delay’: Brussels warns Albania over Trump-linked resort project
entry.content: A spokesperson for the European Commission has said that Tirana must “refrain from actions that could undermine” its bid to join the European Union, amid concerns that a sprawling coastal development linked to Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner is in breach of EU environmental standards.

entry.title: Zelenskyy wants to build up pressure on Russia to force Moscow to peace talks
entry.content: In a post on X, Zelenskyy said that Ukraine had signed a drone deal with Latvia after his first talks with the country's Prime Minister Andris Kulbergs.

entry.title: New Hercule Poirot cast for TV show reboot – and he’s very young
entry.content: Titled 'Hercule', the new six-part series will follow the famous Belgian detective as he solves his first-ever cases before becoming the world’s most iconic sleuth. Now, we know who David Suchet’s successor will be...

entry.title: Rare tour reveals hidden treasures inside Barcelona's Sagrada Familia
entry.content: A rare guided tour of Barcelona’s Sagrada Familia reveals hidden symbols, sacred relics and little-known details embedded within Antoni Gaudí’s world-famous basilica.

entry.title: World Cup border issues continue as Cannavaro questions Uzbekistan security frisk
entry.content: Iran's World Cup ticket allocation has been cancelled, and 13 delegation members remain without visas, forcing the team to base itself in Mexico. Meanwhile, a Somali referee was turned away, an Iraqi striker was held for seven hours in Chicago, and Senegalese players were body-searched on arrival.

entry.title: Israeli director Nadav Lapid, angered by boycott calls, pulls out of Marseille festival
entry.content: The Israeli filmmaker, a critic of Benjamin Netanyahu exiled in France for five years, wonders 'what the hell I'm supposed to do' here if 'my presence is unacceptable and I can simply be erased or swept aside from a film event'.

entry.title: Hot air balloons fill Kazakhstan skies as festival returns
entry.content: One of Kazakhstan's largest ballooning festivals returned to the Almaty region after a two-year break, bringing together 29 hot air balloons and pilots from 12 countries.

entry.title: Top African referee denied entry into the US and will miss World Cup
entry.content: A FIFA spokesperson confirmed in a statement that Artan, who says he was detained for 11 hours at Miami International Airport, would play no part in the World Cup.

entry.title: Amid widespread protests, Albanian island project also sparks flood of fake claims
entry.content: The heated controversy surrounding the multi-billion-euro property development project across various Albanian beauty spots has triggered a stream of misleading online claims.

entry.title: US puts Alibaba, BYD and Baidu on Chinese military blacklist
entry.content: The Pentagon has added 188 Chinese companies to its military blacklist — its biggest ever expansion — in a move that targets household names far beyond the defence sector.

entry.title: EU proposes new sanctions on Russian oil, 'shadow fleet', fisheries and soldiers
entry.content: The new package of sanctions is intended to delay a scheduled review of the price cap on Russian oil, which has surged following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, to avoid granting Moscow economic relief.

entry.title: Bear captured after roaming through Japanese city
entry.content: Authorities in Utsunomiya, north of Tokyo, captured a bear after a multi-day search linked to several sightings. Security camera footage showed the animal running through a shopping arcade, prompting school closures and safety warnings.

entry.title: Host cities ready for kickoff in biggest World Cup ever as fans lament visa bans and ticket prices
entry.content: The United States will host roughly 75% of the 2026 FIFA World Cup with 78 of the 104 matches in the entire tournament taking place in 11 host cities.

entry.title: Travelling to Sardinia? These are the beach rules you need to know to avoid up to €3,500 fines
entry.content: Sardinia continues to cap the number of holidaymakers that can visit its famous beaches in summer.

entry.title: Man arrested after violent stabbing in Belfast, Northern Ireland police say
entry.content: Video posted to social media shows a man stabbing another man several times in the head with a knife in what appears to be an attempted beheading.

entry.title: Icelandair has found the 'world's worst photographer' - and is paying her $50,000
entry.content: A woman named Blanche Mortemard has just been told she is the worst photographer in the world - out of 127,642 people who tried very hard to be exactly that.

entry.title: Zoinks! Fans outraged over four-legged star of Netflix's Scooby-Doo live-action reboot
entry.content: Some people have enough time on their hands to argue over the look of a fictional, talking puppy...

entry.title: As conflicts rage, where are the most peaceful places in the world to travel to right now?
entry.content: The Global Peace Index – the world's most comprehensive annual ranking of peacefulness across 163 countries – has released its top ten for 2026.

entry.title: Philippines earthquake: Residents clear debris amid concerns over aftershocks
entry.content: The Philippines is assessing the impact of a powerful earthquake that struck Mindanao on 8 June. Residents are clearing debris while hospitals continue operating outdoors amid concerns over aftershocks.

entry.title: Von der Leyen's AI envoy pick draws conflict of interest questions
entry.content: Centre-left MEPs are questioning von der Leyen's choice of of Siemens chairman Jim Hagemann Snabe to serve as the EU's industrial AI envoy.

entry.title: Apple lays out its AI with a new Siri: Here's what to know from Tim Cook's last WWDC
entry.content: Apple's unveiling of Siri AI comes after criticism that the company has fallen behind in the AI race.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 9th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 9th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Albania's PM posts AI video of himself in miniskirt in swipe at online influencers
entry.content: In a speech, Rama argued that many influencers make money by promoting themselves on social media while paying no taxes to the state.

entry.title: Spain tourism numbers could soar towards 100 million amid global uncertainty
entry.content: International tourist arrivals rose by 5.2% in April to 26.5 million in the first four months of the year, and the industry now sees topping 100 million visitors as increasingly within reach.

entry.title: Luxury for less: Where to find the best-value five-star hotels in Europe
entry.content: The latest Hotel Price Index has also revealed all the money-saving tips you need to know about when booking.

entry.title: Why the Franco-German FCAS fighter jet project failed
entry.content: Planned as Europe's biggest joint defence project, FCAS has been abandoned after French and German companies failed to resolve years of disputes, despite repeated political intervention.

entry.title: Ukrainian strikes start fires at oil facilities in Russia and Crimea, officials say
entry.content: Daily Russian attacks have intensified in recent months and Ukraine has hit back with its own drone strikes further into Russian territory, saying these are mainly against military and energy facilities.

entry.title: Shaken, not ‘woke’: Idris Elba says that James Bond should ‘just be Bond’
entry.content: Idris Elba has stated that James Bond was “written how he was written for a reason” and suggested that some international audiences would not embrace “a Black male” playing 007.

entry.title: Xi Jinping meets Kim Jong Un as China and North Korea tighten alliance
entry.content: China's President travelled to North Korea for the first time in seven years, underscoring the strategic importance of ties between the two neighbours.

entry.title: Letta: Europe must unite or risk becoming a 'colony' of the US and China
entry.content: The nascent "EU inc" strategy will attempt to complete the single market before the bloc becomes unable to compete with global superpowers.

entry.title: How much is EU trade with the US falling?
entry.content: Exports to the US dropped by nearly a third in the first three months of 2026, as the EU prepares to implement its side of a trade deal with Washington.

entry.title: Trump insists Iran deal is days away as both sides step back from brink
entry.content: Speaking after Monday's exchange of strikes — the biggest blow yet to the April ceasefire in the Iran war — Trump said bombing Iran further would keep the Strait of Hormuz closed for months and cost lives.

entry.title: Belgium: Thousands protest alleged police violence in Brussels
entry.content: Large crowds gathered in Brussels on 8 June to condemn what protesters described as excessive police force during recent demonstrations. The dispute is rooted in planned spending cuts affecting French-speaking education in Belgium

entry.title: New health risks emerge as Europe’s drug market grows more complex, EU agency warns
entry.content: Europe’s drug market is growing more complex, with potent opioids, stronger cannabis and new substances raising health risks, the EU agency warns.

entry.title: Cuba: Powerful offshore earthquake shakes Havana, prompting office evacuation
entry.content: A powerful earthquake struck western Cuba on 8 June, sending people rushing out of buildings in Havana. Witnesses described scenes of confusion as offices were evacuated and workers waited outside for safety checks.

entry.title: Video shows Trump booed at NBA Finals game in New York
entry.content: US President Donald Trump was booed by some spectators as he attended Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden in New York. He appeared during the national anthem before the matchup between the Knicks and the Spurs.

entry.title: Bogs 'almost like a minefield': Europe's wetlands gain military importance
entry.content: Peatlands are more than carbon sinks and havens for rare species. Amid rising security tensions, they are seen across Europe as natural barriers to attackers.

entry.title: NASA astronaut films Southern Lights from SpaceX Dragon spacecraft
entry.content: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir shared a stunning timelapse of the Southern Lights filmed from a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft. The aurora, triggered by recent solar activity, appears to dance across the atmosphere above Antarctica.

entry.title: Ukraine: Strikes in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy kill three civilians and injure dozens
entry.content: Ukraine reported fresh civilian casualties on 8 June after Russian drone strikes hit residential areas in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy.

entry.title: Seven-time F1 world champion Lewis Hamilton facing backlash over anti-billionaire comments
entry.content: A three-year-old interview with Lewis Hamilton has resurfaced online, which has prompted fresh conversation about society’s wealth gap, inequality, and for some, tone-deaf hypocrisy. Especially considering who his date was in Monaco last weekend...

entry.title: Watch: Inside the deepest diplomatic crisis between Poland and Ukraine
entry.content: A recent military designation has sparked a major diplomatic crisis between Warsaw and Kyiv, exposing deeply conflicting national narratives. What has happened?

entry.title: 'Heads must roll': France demands judicial overhaul after murder of 11-year-old girl
entry.content: The main suspect, Jérôme Barella, had faced multiple complaints for sexual violence against minors but was never investigated or questioned - until now.

entry.title: Books, authors and readers: a day at the Lisbon Book Fair
entry.content: In Lisbon, June is about more than the popular saints’ festivities: it is also the month when Parque Eduardo VII turns into the country’s biggest bookshop. Ninety-six years after its first edition in 1930, the Book Fair still draws huge crowds. We went to find out why.

entry.title: Around 20,000 people displaced by Philippine earthquake that killed at least 37
entry.content: The Philippines is often hit by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions due to its location on the Pacific “Ring of Fire,” an arc of seismic faults around the ocean.

entry.title: Newsletter: Squeezing Moscow's war chest
entry.content: Also in this newsletter: A group of MEPs are up in arms over planned technical talks between EU and Taliban officials on the return of migrants to Afghanistan.

entry.title: The EU’s migration pact takes full effect: are countries ready to deliver?
entry.content: Co-legislators agreed on “return hubs” on 1 June, preparing to rollout the Migration and Asylum Pact of 12 June. Stricter border controls, uniform asylum rules, and a new digital monitoring system will crack down on illegal migration. But are member states ready for the new rules?

entry.title: Europe Today: Former Italian PM Letta on Euronews as Iran halts attacks on Israel
entry.content: Former Italian prime minister and President of the Jacques Delors Institute Enrico Letta speaks to Euronews. We also cover the Middle East, where Iran announced the end of military operations against Israel but warned of “a more crushing” response if it resumes strikes on Lebanon or its territory.

entry.title: China's Xi vows to bring ties with North Korea to 'new heights' during rare visit
entry.content: It was Xi's first visit to North Korea in seven years. Xi and Kim last met in Beijing in September after viewing a military parade alongside Russian President Vladimir Putin and other foreign leaders.

entry.title: Businesses and unions unite against Swiss immigration cap ahead of Sunday referendum
entry.content: The initiative faces broad opposition across the government, parliament and business sector, but opinion polls suggest the vote could be tight.

entry.title: No phones allowed: Phoebe Bridgers takes ‘The Lost Tour’ to Europe
entry.content: Bridgers joins artists like Bob Dylan, Jack White and Ghost in outright banning phones from live shows.

entry.title: Trump booed by crowd during national anthem prior to NBA Finals in New York
entry.content: The Secret Service deployed counter-drone technology as part of its operation to protect the US leader, who has faced three alleged assassination attempts in less than two years.

entry.title: How cyber criminals are taking advantage of the FIFA World Cup
entry.content: From fake websites to malicious calendar invitations, here is how cyber attackers have already been taking advantage of the FIFA World Cup hype.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 9th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 9th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Peaks, lakes and coastal views: Here are the best trails in Europe to hike this summer
entry.content: Lace up your hiking boots and swap crowded city breaks for volcanic landscapes, dramatic peaks and coastal trails across the continent.

entry.title: A century without Gaudí: Barcelona honours the architect who turned the city into a work of art
entry.content: Gaudí shaped the iconic architecture that lures tourists to Barcelona, but his magnum opus, the Sagrada Família, will not be completed in 2026; on the centenary of his death, Pope Leo XIV will be there.

entry.title: France and Germany abandon joint fighter jet project as companies unable to reach agreement
entry.content: The project was seen as a key test of European efforts to work more closely on defence as they seek to present a united front in the face of a hostile Russia at a time of souring ties with the United States.

entry.title: ICC chief prosecutor suspended pending proceedings on sexual misconduct allegations
entry.content: The 56-year-old Karim Khan is facing allegations of sexual misconduct with a female aide, in a scandal that has dragged on for more than two years. He has steadfastly denied any wrongdoing.

entry.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon as attacks persist despite Iran threat
entry.content: Iran has warned that it will resume attacks if Israel continues striking Lebanon, but Israeli leaders on Monday insisted they would not be deterred.

entry.title: US judge blocks Trump plan for $100,000 visa fee
entry.content: The H-1B visa programme, in place in its current form since 1990, allows for 85,000 visas each year, including 20,000 allocated to applicants with advanced degrees.

entry.title: Danish footballer Christian Eriksen 'doing well' after collapse
entry.content: Eriksen is recovering at home with his family after collapsing during a game against Ukraine on Sunday.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | June 8th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this June 8th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: The Sun and the Moon: Saatchi Gallery's ambitious new summer show turns its gaze to the sky
entry.content: From a giant glowing Sun and immersive installations that can't be photographed, to ancient Arctic snow goggles and textiles inspired by the Apollo missions, the exhibition spans centuries of human imagination.

entry.title: Czech forest turns into Middle Earth for 750-player fantasy battle event
entry.content: A forest in the Czech Republic was turned into a real-life version of The Hobbit as around 750 fantasy fans took part in a large-scale live-action battle inspired by the Battle of the Five Armies.

entry.title: Wizz Air announces Starlink wifi deal as other budget rivals hold back
entry.content: The Hungarian budget carrier will roll out satellite internet across its fleet from 2027, as Ryanair and EasyJet continue to resist the technology over cost concerns.

entry.title: Houthis join Iran war fight, threatening Red Sea shipping amid Hormuz closure
entry.content: Yemen's Houthis declared a ban on Israeli shipping in the Red Sea and claimed a missile strike on Israel, with the Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz blockades now simultaneously threatening roughly a third of the world's seaborne oil and gas.

entry.title: Hundreds dressed as Charlie Chaplin attempt world record in Switzerland
entry.content: More than 400 people dressed as Charlie Chaplin's iconic character, The Tramp, gathered in Switzerland on Sunday in an attempt to break the record for the largest assembly of Chaplin lookalikes.

entry.title: Paraguay, Palau, Uzbekistan: The lesser-trod destinations travellers are flocking to this year
entry.content: The latest UN Tourism report has highlighted all the ways travel is changing in 2026.

entry.title: Kremlin-backed artist uses AI deepfakes and stirs controversy
entry.content: Kremlin-backed artist Yaroslav Dronov, known as Shaman, has made extensive use of AI deepfakes in his latest music video 'Mother Russia' ('Rossiya – Mama'). This has stirred much controversy.

entry.title: This stunning cave hotel just opened next to one of Cappadocia’s most iconic landmarks
entry.content: Rooms at the Elika Cave Suites Cappadocia come with private pools, whirlpool baths or even a Turkish bath and sauna.

entry.title: Public ownership of AI? US officials eye stake in tech revolution
entry.content: From Washington to Brussels, the fight over who controls artificial intelligence is reaching a turning point, and history suggests the public has always had to wrestle essential technology from completely private control.

entry.title: Talay Riley stabbed to death: Celebrities lead tributes to singer-songwriter
entry.content: Talay Riley, 35, was stabbed to death on 5 June in Silvertown, London. Artists have paid tribute to a unique talent.

entry.title: The world’s best cities for food have been revealed by Time Out – and four are in Europe
entry.content: Based on a survey of more than 24,000 residents across 150 cities, this is the ultimate list for foodies looking for their next travel destination.

entry.title: The NBA Finals are back at Madison Square Garden and Donald Trump is crashing the party
entry.content: Donald Trump won't be the only famous figure in attendance when the NBA Finals return to New York for the first time since 1999.

entry.title: Donald Trump’s Freedom 250 / birthday cage fight event snubbed by celebrities
entry.content: Donald Trump’s extravagant 80th birthday cage fight on the White House lawn is proving to be celebrity repellent... Adam Sandler, Jared Leto and Dwayne ‘The Rock’ Johnson are among the celebrities who have declined invites to the UFC Freedom 250 event set to take place on 14 June.

entry.title: Apple to make AI software push at upcoming Silicon Valley conference
entry.content: The tech giant is expected to launch new AI integration into its software and changes to the voice assistant, Siri.

entry.title: Hundreds march in memory of slain French schoolgirl Lyhanna
entry.content: Hundreds of residents gathered in the southwestern French town of Fleurance on Saturday to take part in a silent march honouring Lyhanna, the 11-year-old schoolgirl whose death has shocked the nation.

entry.title: Date nights, bookshops and perfect Prishtina: Dua Lipa shares her European travel recommendations
entry.content: Are your summer holiday plans in need of New Rules? Dua Lipa has curated a list of where to go, eat and sight-see with Google Maps that you can download now.

entry.title: Front desk vibes, back alley dreams: Feast your eyes on Denmark’s 2026 Mullet Championship
entry.content: When life gives you lemons, grow a mullet. The 2026 Mullet Championship took place on Saturday in Copenhagen, where 12 well-coiffed competitors fought for the title of most powerful mullet...

entry.title: Patrick Bruel in police custody over sexual assault allegations
entry.content: Patrick Bruel has been questioned since Monday morning over newly uncovered matters now before the courts, the Nanterre public prosecutor’s office said.

entry.title: Iranians protest outside World Cup stadium ahead of Iran match
entry.content: More than 200 members of the Iranian diaspora gathered outside Los Angeles Stadium on Sunday, waving Iran's pre-revolutionary "Lion and Sun" flags and protesting against the Iranian government ahead of Iran's first match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: ‘He was the best of us’: Heartfelt tributes paid to Buffy star Anthony Head, dead at 72
entry.content: Best known for his role as Giles on the cult TV show Buffy The Vampire Slayer, Anthony Head died at the age of 72. Over the weekend, his co-stars and friends paid heartfelt tributes to a performer who will be sorely missed.

entry.title: The world's most travelled people gather in Portugal for annual meet-up
entry.content: In Portugal, the Most Traveled People (MTP) community brought together around 200 die-hard travellers, half of whom have already visited every country in the world recognised by the UN.

entry.title: Aliens, Pan-Africanism and Olivia Rodrigo: What to see, do or hear this week in Europe
entry.content: Steven Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day' beams into the box office while Olivia Rodrigo sings about love making us crazy. Here's what to look out for this week.

entry.title: Pope Leo XIV meets Antonio Banderas, watches flamenco performance in Madrid
entry.content: Pope Leo XIV attended a cultural event in Madrid featuring performances, speeches and appearances by leading figures from the worlds of arts, culture and sport.

entry.title: Travelling to Belgium? An expert shares their top tips for visitors
entry.content: Belgium expert Angela Dansby reveals her top travel tips for the country, including attractions to see and the food and drink to try.

entry.title: Danish footballer Christian Eriksen 'conscious' after collapsing on field
entry.content: The former Tottenham and Manchester United midfielder went down on the pitch in Odense, Denmark’s third-largest city. According to reports, he remains conscious, and the match has been abandoned

entry.title: German Zverev wins his first Grand Slam, beating Italian Cobolli
entry.content: Zverev’s serve remained on point at crucial moments, and he broke Cobolli at 4–5 in the fourth set to force the tiebreak. He then dominated the fifth set with two breaks and consolidated both.

entry.title: 2026 World Cup: five key facts about FIFA's biggest ever tournament
entry.content: The 2026 World Cup will be the first with 48 teams, 104 matches and three host nations. With record figures, extreme heat risks and Super Bowl-style spectacle, the North American tournament promises to break the mould.

entry.title: Cannes winner Jafar Panahi faces prison and travel ban from Iran
entry.content: On Sunday, Mostafa Nili, lawyer for Jafar Panahi, announced that a Tehran Revolutionary Court has rejected the objections and fully upheld the in-absentia verdict against the prominent Iranian filmmaker.

entry.title: Hitler's 70 km ghost motorway: Germany's longest abandoned autobahn
entry.content: Route 46 is Germany’s longest abandoned motorway project. It owes less to transport efficiency than to Nazi-era construction ideology and propaganda.

entry.title: Inside Amazon’s busiest European warehouse, where robots, lasers and humans deliver the future
entry.content: Amazon says robots will make work safer and deliveries faster. Euronews Next saw them in action.

entry.title: 13 floors, 1,240 rooms: largest Baltic Sea hotel opens – but there is a catch
entry.content: Thirteen floors and 1,240 rooms: a mega-hotel on the Baltic Sea coast is about to open – but this is where the real snag lies.

entry.title: Thousands join record-breaking human wave attempt in Mexico City ahead of World Cup
entry.content: Thousands of people gathered in Mexico City to attempt a Guinness World Record for the world's largest human wave, days before the start of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

entry.title: Guadalajara: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entry.content: Mexico's second-largest city is hosting its third men's World Cup after previously staging matches in 1970 and 1986.

entry.title: Toronto: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entry.content: Canada's largest city will host six World Cup matches, including the co-host nation’s opening fixture.

entry.title: NASA aircraft breaks sound barrier in first supersonic test flight
entry.content: The milestone comes after a busy testing period for NASA. Since the first flight on 28 October 2025, the team has completed 16 flights in the last 90 days

entry.title: Tennis: Mirra Andreeva wins women's Roland Garros final
entry.content: The world No. 8 duly brushed aside Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska, winning in straight sets, 6-3 6-2. Mirra Andreeva - nineteen years old - is the youngest winner since Monica Seles

entry.title: Dua Lipa and Callum Turner celebrate with guests at Sicily wedding party
entry.content: Newlyweds Dua Lipa and Callum Turner were seen greeting guests and celebrating at a wedding party in Palermo, Sicily, as part of a weekend of festivities following their recent London wedding.

entry.title: 'Mehrzweckeier': dig at Merz for youth word of the year? Voting under way
entry.content: 'Mehrzweckeier': youth word of the year as a protest? Citizens can now vote on it officially. What began as criticism of conscription reform is now aimed at Friedrich Merz. Insult or free speech?

entry.title: Lazio Maremma cowboys: tradition and innovation at Canale Monterano's Riarto
entry.content: For centuries, the buttero was central to livestock and rural life in the Maremma between Lazio and Tuscany. Though the trade is fading, new horsemen keep the region's history and identity alive.

entry.title: Spain welcomes Pope Leo XIV on landmark six-day tour: key events and highlights
entry.content: Millions are expected to attend events across Spain as Leo XIV combines religious celebrations with high-profile political meetings.

entry.title: Philadelphia: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entry.content: Philadelphia will host five World Cup group games as well as a Round of 16 clash on 4 July – exactly 250 years after the Declaration of Independence was signed in the historic city.

entry.title: The Baltic states need more drone-detection radars. Europe's defence bottlenecks may slow them down
entry.content: The Baltic States, like the rest of Europe, are grappling with equipment shortages for the key technologies that will make it easier to respond to drone incursions, experts say.

entry.title: Vancouver: A travel guide to the 2026 World Cup host city
entry.content: BC Place in downtown Vancouver will host seven World Cup fixtures – including two of Canada's group games.

entry.title: Patagonia vs Pattie Gonia: Why is the clothing giant suing the famous drag queen?
entry.content: American clothing company Patagonia has filed a trademark infringement lawsuit against drag queen and environmental activist Pattie Gonia, prompting a public dispute over branding, activism, and intellectual property rights.

entry.title: Qatari artist designs Pierre Gasly's helmet for Canadian Grand Prix
entry.content: The design, created through the 2026 Year of Culture partnership involving Qatar, Canada and Mexico, combines symbols from both Qatar and Canada.

entry.title: Portugal to deploy over 300 more police officers at airports this summer to curb queues
entry.content: The measure comes in the wake of bottlenecks recently reported at border control in some Portuguese airports, with Lisbon particularly affected.

entry.title: ‘None of us could have predicted this’: Scottish island closes on Sundays after visitor surge
entry.content: After featuring in a BBC show, Ulva received “unprecedented interest” from holidaymakers, according to local ferry operators.

entry.title: Tributes paid to MOBO Awards founder and cultural pioneer Kanya King, dead at 57
entry.content: MOBO Awards founder Kanya King championed Black musicians' contribution to British culture and funded the first Music Of Black Origin awards in 1996. Heartfelt tributes have been posted, remembering her as a "visionary" and "a pioneer" who "changed the face of culture and music.”

entry.title: Germany: Bavarian lake procession marks centuries-old boat tradition for Corpus Christi
entry.content: Hundreds of worshippers gathered in Bavaria on 4 June for Seehausen's traditional Corpus Christi lake procession. The event, held annually since 1935, combines religious devotion with local heritage on the waters of Staffelsee.

entry.title: Euronews Culture's Film of the Week: 'Backrooms' - Liminal space horror you'll want to get lost in
entry.content: Released last week in the US and UK, this year’s breakout horror hit finally heads to European cinemas. It was worth the wait.

entry.title: Bayeux Tapestry set to go on show at British Museum
entry.content: The medieval masterpiece will be displayed at the London museum from September 2026 to July 2027 to mark the 1,000th anniversary of William the Conqueror.

entry.title: Counter-drone technology showcased as Europe develops plans for 'drone wall'
entry.content: Drone makers and defence firms showcased the latest drone detection and counter-drone technologies at a major industry event in Denmark, as European countries step up efforts to address growing security concerns linked to UAV incursions and modern warfare.

entry.title: Lebanon: UN clears debris in Dibbine after Israeli withdrawal
entry.content: UN peacekeepers and heavy machinery entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine on 5 June to clear debris left by recent fighting.

entry.title: Romania: TV reporter flees live on air after drone explosion in Constanța
entry.content: A live television report from Romania's Black Sea port of Constanța was abruptly interrupted on 5 June after a maritime drone exploded nearby, heightening concerns over the regional impact of the war in Ukraine.

entry.title: 'Moved to tears': Winners of the 2026 World Food Photography Awards revealed
entry.content: From Soviet spas to sacred rivers, this World Food Photography Awards offer a glimpse into the many ways food influences our lives around the globe.

entry.title: Jack White’s panned exhibition: Which musicians found success in fine art?
entry.content: Jack White’s debut exhibition has divided opinion. Here’s how other music icons fared in the court of public opinion and the eyes of critics.

entry.title: Anthropic calls for ‘brake pedal’ before AI develops itself without human oversight
entry.content: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark said AI agents might soon be able to build and train models themselves and, if that happens, humans could lose control over AI systems.

entry.title: Trump ‘oil painting’ football image goes viral and sparks Pride Month jokes
entry.content: Timing is everything, and an image reportedly posted by Donald Trump on his Truth Social platform has caused a stir, considering June is Pride Month. An inadvertent celebration?

entry.title: 'Jumanji' and 'Top Gun: Maverick' actor James Handy stabbed to death aged 81
entry.content: Handy enjoyed a career spanning five decades, with notable roles including the exterminator in Jumanji, Detective Alan Cross in The X-Files, and bartender Jimmy in Top Gun: Maverick.

entry.title: All you need is... an official date: Inaugural Global Beatles Day announced
entry.content: Unofficially celebrated since 2009, The Beatles and Apple Corps Ltd. have announced that the first official Global Beatles Day will take place later this month. It will include online and in-person events around the world...

entry.title: Venezuela's Dancing Devils revive centuries-old Corpus Christi rite
entry.content: Children making their First Communion were accompanied by masked "Dancing Devils" in Tarmas, Venezuela. The centuries-old Corpus Christi celebration symbolises the triumph of good over evil and remains a key cultural tradition.

entry.title: Thousands join Jerusalem pride parade under tight security
entry.content: Thousands joined Jerusalem Pride parade on 4 June, under heavy security, marching for equality and acceptance in a deeply conservative, religiously significant city.

entry.title: Denmark probes death of humpback whale that captivated Germany
entry.content: Denmark has launched a post-mortem examination on Timmy, a humpback whale whose struggle to return to the sea captured public attention in Germany and beyond. Scientists hope to determine why the animal died weeks after being returned to open waters.

entry.title: Africa’s hottest new safari lodges are redefining luxury travel in the wild
entry.content: From helicopter arrivals in Zambia to design-led lodges in Tanzania and ultra-luxury retreats in Botswana, a new wave of safari openings across Africa is transforming the classic game drive holiday into something far more immersive, stylish and experience-led.

entry.title: Why are European governments reevaluating their agreements with US defence tech contractor Palantir?
entry.content: Critics have flagged Palantir for their links to militaries involved in ongoing conflicts and ICE immigration enforcement in the United States.

entry.title: The dark side of the 2026 World Cup: Record-breaking emissions and thousands of flights
entry.content: A report warns that the tournament in the US, Canada and Mexico could produce twice the emissions of previous editions.

entry.title: Gaza city residents survey destruction after overnight Israeli airstrike
entry.content: Gaza residents surveyed extensive damage on 4 June after an overnight Israeli airstrike hit an apartment in the Sheikh Radwan neighbourhood of Gaza City.

entry.title: Gaza: Families mourn victims after overnight Israeli strikes that killed at least nine
entry.content: Families gathered in Gaza City to mourn relatives killed on 4 June after overnight Israeli airstrikes in Gaza City, according to local hospitals.

entry.title: Iran war, strikes, EES: Why the number of people flying in Europe is dropping
entry.content: An industry group says that while marginal, the decline “marks a significant milestone” for Europe’s aviation sector.

entry.title: 10 iconic moments that shaped World Cup history
entry.content: The 2026 World Cup kicks off next week in Mexico City, where joint hosts Mexico play South Africa.

entry.title: Love night trains? This new map shows all the sleeper services running across Europe in 2026
entry.content: The 2026 edition shows five new connections already running or lauching this year.

entry.title: Travel back to the time of the great Arctic expeditions with a stay in this 19th-century style cabin
entry.content: HX Expeditions is marking the 130th anniversary of its landmark expedition cruise by unveiling the 1896 Cabin.

entry.title: France unveils 33 ‘palaces’ for 2026 as six new hotels join elite list
entry.content: The latest collection of palace hotels have been revealed, with six properties joining France’s most exclusive hospitality club.

entry.title: From AI robot baristas to military drones: The weird and wonderful tech at Computex 2026
entry.content: Euronews Next spotlights the most innovative and intriguing tech at the conference in Taiwan.

entry.title: Amazon pledges €10 billion for Europe with 25,000 new jobs and warehouse robots
entry.content: Amazon is making one of its biggest new bets on Europe, announcing more than €10 billion in investments to expand and modernise its warehouse and delivery network across the continent over the coming years.

entry.title: This Finnish ski resort is launching a summer-long treasure hunt for a €20,000 gold bar
entry.content: Visitors will have longer to search in the daylight as Levi experiences the midnight sun from late May to the end of July.

entry.title: New York mayor Mamdani and Bastian Schweinsteiger unveil World Cup trophy
entry.content: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former Germany captain Bastian Schweinsteiger welcomed the World Cup trophy to New York as part of FIFA's official Trophy Tour ahead of the 2026 tournament.

entry.title: Revealed: The most beautiful restaurants in Europe for 2026
entry.content: The Prix Versailles architecture and design awards have revealed the World’s Most Beautiful Restaurants for 2026. Five European venues made the prestigious list, celebrating restaurants where striking interiors are every bit as memorable as the food.

entry.title: France: Protesters rally outside CNews TV over alleged Kremlin influence
entry.content: France witnessed renewed debate over foreign influence in the media on 3 June as dozens of protesters gathered outside CNews headquarters in Paris to denounce Ksenia Fedorova, the former head of RT France.
